title: Michael Pollan's 'A World Appears': Unraveling the Mystery of Consciousness
content:
Michael Pollan's latest book, A World Appears: A Journey Into Consciousness, takes readers on a profound exploration of the mind. The author is upfront about what his book will not do, refusing to settle the age-old debate about subjective experience. He avoids taking a definitive stance between those who reduce consciousness to neurons and those who suspect something more ineffable is at work. Even for the best-selling author, the mystery of the subject remains difficult to crack.

By his count, there are currently 106 competing theories of consciousness. Instead of making a case for any single one, Pollan takes us along on a quest for understanding that pushes the reader to become more conscious. He emphasizes that it is miraculous how a world appears every time we wake from sleep. No one has yet fully explained how or why this phenomenon occurs.

Pollan reminds us that plants also possess a form of sentience, sensing their surroundings and responding accordingly. Moving on, he defines feelings as physical processes that produce mental experiences, and thought as the streaming content of our waking hours. Through a travelogue approach, Pollan explores the differences between sentience, feelings, thought, and the self. Despite his deep investigation, the very essence of human consciousness remains beautifully unknown.
